prompt:
Implement Phase 3 (Google Sheets Sync) of the Balance plan. Read docs/PLAN.md and CLAUDE.md for full context.

## Scope

This phase adds the sync abstraction layer and Google Sheets as the first sync backend. It also adds a settings UI for connecting a Google account.

## 1. SyncBackend interface (client/src/sync/interface.ts)

Create the sync abstraction:

```typescript
export interface SyncBackend {
  connect(): Promise<void>;
  pushEntries(entries: LogEntry[]): Promise<void>;
  pullEntries(since: number): Promise<LogEntry[]>;
  pushMetrics(metrics: Metric[]): Promise<void>;
  pullMetrics(since: number): Promise<Metric[]>;
  disconnect(): Promise<void>;
  isConnected(): boolean;
}
```

## 2. Google OAuth client-side flow (client/src/sync/google-auth.ts)

Implement Google OAuth using the Google Identity Services (GIS) library:
- Use the token model (implicit grant flow) — no backend needed
- Scopes needed: https://www.googleapis.com/auth/spreadsheets
- Store the access token in memory (and refresh on expiry)
- The OAuth client ID will need to be configured — for now use an environment variable VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID
- Add the GIS script tag to index.html

## 3. Google Sheets sync backend (client/src/sync/google-sheets.ts)

Implement SyncBackend for Google Sheets:
- On connect(): authenticate with Google, then find or create a spreadsheet named 'Balance Data'
- Use two sheets/tabs in the spreadsheet:
  - 'Logs' sheet: columns timestamp, metricId, metricName, value
  - 'Metrics' sheet: columns id, name, leftLabel, rightLabel, steps, order, enabled, schedule
- pushEntries(): append new log entries to the Logs sheet
- pullEntries(since): read rows from Logs sheet where timestamp > since
- pushMetrics(): write all metric definitions to Metrics sheet (overwrite)
- pullMetrics(): read all rows from Metrics sheet
- Use Google Sheets API v4 REST endpoints directly (fetch calls with auth token), no SDK needed
- Handle token expiry gracefully

## 4. Wire sync into the store (client/src/store.ts)

- Add a syncBackend state to the store
- After recording a log, if sync is connected, push the new entry
- On app load, if sync was previously connected, try to reconnect and pull
- Store sync connection state in localStorage (just a boolean flag, not the token)

## 5. Settings UI

Add a simple settings section (could be a gear icon in the header that toggles a settings panel):
- Show sync status: 'Not connected' / 'Connected to Google Sheets'
- 'Connect Google Sheets' button that triggers OAuth flow
- 'Disconnect' button when connected
- 'Sync Now' button to manually trigger push/pull
- Keep it minimal — just enough to connect and see status

## 6. E2e test

Add client/e2e/sync-ui.spec.ts:
- Test that the settings panel opens/closes
- Test that the 'Connect Google Sheets' button is visible when not connected
- Do NOT test actual Google OAuth (that requires real credentials)
- Screenshot the settings panel

## Verification

- npm run lint and npm run build must pass
- npm run test:e2e must pass (new test + existing tests)
- The Google Sheets sync won't be testable without a real OAuth client ID, but the code should compile and the UI should render correctly

Create PR titled 'Phase 3: Google Sheets sync with OAuth'
